_meta.purpose: Blood parasites — microfilariae (filariae) + trypanosomes/leishmania on blood film -> disease. Keys are YOLO class names (snake_case) from the microfilaria/blood-parasite detector. Resolves each to its disease + significance. Malaria/Babesia are covered by the dedicated malaria detector and the PBS blood_parasite classes. Decision support only; a microscopist speciates.
_meta.source: CDC DPDx + WHO filariasis references. Curated for JORINOVA NEXUS.

wuchereria_bancrofti.name: Wuchereria bancrofti microfilaria
wuchereria_bancrofti.note: Sheathed; gently curved; nuclei do NOT reach tip. Nocturnal periodicity — sample ~22:00-02:00. Chronic -> lymphoedema/elephantiasis. Treat: DEC (+ albendazole).

brugia_malayi.name: Brugia malayi microfilaria
brugia_malayi.note: Sheathed; kinky; TWO separate terminal nuclei at tip. Nocturnal periodicity. Treat: DEC.

loa_loa.name: Loa loa microfilaria
loa_loa.note: Sheathed; nuclei extend to tip. DIURNAL periodicity — sample midday. Exclude high load before DEC/ivermectin (encephalopathy risk).

mansonella_perstans.name: Mansonella perstans microfilaria
mansonella_perstans.note: UNsheathed; small; nuclei to blunt tip. No periodicity. Often mild/asymptomatic.

onchocerca_volvulus.name: Onchocerca volvulus microfilaria (skin snip)
onchocerca_volvulus.note: Skin/nodule, NOT blood. Unsheathed. Ocular involvement -> blindness. Treat: ivermectin (avoid if Loa co-infection with high load).

trypanosoma_brucei.name: Trypanosoma brucei trypomastigote
trypanosoma_brucei.note: Extracellular flagellate in plasma. CNS stage fatal if untreated. Urgent.

leishmania.name: Leishmania amastigote
leishmania.note: Intracellular amastigotes (Leishman-Donovan bodies) in macrophages — marrow/splenic aspirate. Visceral (kala-azar) fatal if untreated.

microfilaria.name: Microfilaria (species unspecified)
microfilaria.note: Assess sheath + tail nuclei + periodicity to speciate (Wuchereria/Brugia/Loa/Mansonella).
